description: §8.3 / §52.1 SQL `.all()` array-shape hydrate (RUNTIME, serverDb driver) — the explicit `.all()` form resolves to Row[] and hydrates the client cell @rows as an ARRAY. Distinct from the `.get()` sibling: three seeded rows hydrate as a length-3 array (#count reads @rows.length → 3). Pure data-flow (no WHERE / ordering).
rationale: §8.3 (result-shape table): `.all()` resolves to Row[] — ALL matching rows as an array (identical shape to a bare `?{}`). §12.2 / §52.1: loadAll escalates to server and round-trips; the returned array hydrates @rows. Three seeded widgets → a length-3 array; #count (reading @rows.length) reads 3 — the array-shape proof (contrast the `.get()` single-object sibling). Asserted purely as data-flow: the seeded rows reach the client as an array of the seeded count (no WHERE-filter / ordering — BLOCKED engine surface).
